body: Indonesia's trade balance fell 20.71% in January-September 2024, with a deficit of $15.04 billion in the oil and gas sector and a total trade balance of -$21.98 billion, compared to -$27.72 billion in the same period last year. In September 2024, the trade balance narrowed 0.8% to $3.25 billion, with oil and gas exports contributing $1.16 billion and non-oil gas exports contributing $20.91 billion.
title: Indonesia's trade balance declines 20.71% in January-September 2024 due to increased deficit in oil and gas sector.

body: Indonesian Securities Rating Agency has downgraded PT PP Properti Tbk's rating to "IDS" due to default on its obligations, and its shelf registration bonds II Phase IV to "IDD" from "IDBB-", citing the company's debt standstill and inability to make payments. The agency also lowered ratings for the company's shelf registration bonds II Phase I and III to "IDCCC" from "IDBB-", reflecting a high likelihood that the company will not fulfill its obligation to pay bond coupons when they mature.
title: Indonesian Securities Rating Agency Downgrades PT PP Properti's Bond Ratings Due to Default Status.

body: The Indonesian government will hold an auction for 7 series of government sukuk on October 22, 2024, to fulfill part of its 2024 financing target, with rewards offered in the form of discounts for SPNS series and ranging from 4.87% to 6.87% for PBS series. This will be the 21st government sukuk auction held since the beginning of 2024, following the previous auction on October 8, 2024, which saw IDR 14.81 trillion in bids.
title: Indonesian Government to Hold Auction for 7 Series of Sharia Securities on October 22, 2024

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) rose to 7,716.32 at 11:30 WIB, up from 7,665.00 at the start of the session, driven by gains in large-cap issuers such as PT Barito Renewables Energy, PT Chandra Asri Pacific, and PT Bank Central Asia. The JCI's strengthening trend is seen as a market response to the announcement of ministerial candidates, particularly in the economy and investment fields, ahead of Prabowo Subianto's inauguration on Sunday.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Rises Amid Speculation on Ministerial Candidates and Upcoming Presidential Inauguration

body: PT Global Digital Niaga Tbk, the issuer of Djarum Group, will issue 4.9 billion new shares, equivalent to 4.14% of its fully paid capital, at a nominal value of IDR 250 per share, to raise IDR 2.25 trillion in fresh funds. The new shares will be taken up by PT Lingkarmulia Indah, an affiliated party, and are scheduled to be listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on October 23, 2024.
title: Blibli.com's Parent Company to Raise IDR 2.25 Trillion through Issuance of 4.9 Billion New Shares

body: PT Link Net Tbk, a telecommunications services company, has secured an additional IDR 1 trillion credit facility from Deutsche Bank AG, bringing the total credit facility to IDR 2 trillion, which will be available until November 30, 2025, to finance the company's general needs. This development comes after Link Net signed a IDR 12.94 trillion contract with its parent company, PT XL Axiata Tbk, in September, which includes leasing hybrid fiber coaxial fiber home networks and selling its residential business unit.
title: PT Link Net Tbk secures IDR 1 trillion credit facility from Deutsche Bank to finance general company needs.

body: PT Kalbe Farma Tbk has inaugurated a radioisotope factory, which is expected to support cancer detection and strengthen the company's position in the prescription drug segment. As of June 2024, the company has cash and cash equivalents of IDR 3.85 trillion and total assets of IDR 28.57 trillion, with total equity of IDR 23.54 trillion.
title: Kalbe Farma inaugurates radioisotope factory to produce cancer-detecting fludeoxyglucose.

body: Ferry Teja, an individual investor, acquired a 5.9% stake in PT Fimperkasa Utama Tbk, a general construction company, by purchasing 23.59 million shares, while Matrix Company Limited, a foreign investor, sold 5.32 million shares of PT Samator Indo Gas Tbk, an industrial gas company. Other notable transactions include PT Sinarmas Cakrawala acquiring 6.75 million shares of its financial services subsidiary, PT Sinarmas Multiartha Tbk, and PT Sekar Laut Tbk selling 207,000 shares of its own company.
title: Multiple companies and investors acquire and divest shares in various Indonesian firms across different industries.
